summary: Story, Architecture, canonical Spec, topology replay plan, implementation, and focused E2E agree on the guarded one-command closure contract; the closure composes rather than duplicates the three merged predecessor surfaces.
inspection_summary: Confirmed HEAD 66131d96a869fc317519a66c13ea0e3b0b8d3e1d; read the Story, Architecture, canonical Spec and test/replay plans, all predecessor Stories, the closure/run-session/CLI/runtime/review code and the predecessor-boundary E2E. Ran the focused unit plus production-shaped E2E suite (20/20 passed), checked the branch diff for whitespace errors, and compared changed integration seams with the documented predecessor ownership boundaries.
inspection_evidence: .vibepro/reviews/story-vibepro-one-command-pr-ready-closure/planning_spec/review-request-spec_consistency.md; focused test command passed 20/20 at HEAD 66131d96a869fc317519a66c13ea0e3b0b8d3e1d; test/e2e/story-vibepro-one-command-pr-ready-closure-runtime.spec.ts scenario:S-005 asserts the staged predecessor boundary; git diff --check 118b68091b296f6bb8c4414cfc5eadb69ed73dc0..HEAD exited 0.
judgment_delta: Initial concern: the closure might duplicate the autonomous DAG, production connector, or independent-review implementations. Final conclusion: Architecture assigns those modules their existing authorities, src/one-command-pr-ready-closure.js only supplies injected action callbacks, and scenario:S-005 independently checks PR #372/#377/#382 as canonical predecessor evidence.

Initial concern: the new final closure could weaken the current-HEAD or human-authority contracts across CLI, run-session, and gate artifacts. Final conclusion: the Spec/Architecture and focused tests agree that only final_prepare may emit pr_ready from current-HEAD readiness, while PR create/merge/waiver/external effects remain rejected or explicit human operations.

Initial concern: regression or output-surface coverage might be limited to a new happy path. Final conclusion: the focused suite covers available-provider commit/review/repair replay, typed capability and runtime stops, resume, cancellation, CLI status/rendering, and the staged post-PR record; all 20 targeted tests passed.

Initial concern: final Story/roadmap completion might contradict OCR-S-8's active pre-PR state. Final conclusion: the Story, Architecture, Spec S-OCR-6, test plan, and scenario:S-005 consistently distinguish the initial active pre-PR candidate from the separately recorded post-PR delivery closure at this HEAD.
